description: The StaticTexture2D Component stores a reference to a static asset representing a 2D Texture.

fields.URL: The Asset URI that references the raw texture data
fields.DirectLoad: Whether to load the image directly from it's URI source and to not cache it locally.
fields.ForceExactVariant: Only allow one variant of the texture to exist, part of Asset Variant System.
fields.PreferredFormat: The format to use for texture compression rather than the auto picked one. See Texture Compression Formats for more details.
fields.PreferredProfile: The color profile to use rather than the auto picked one. (usually linear)
fields.IsNormalMap: This texture should be interpreted as a Normal Map
fields.PowerOfTwoAlignThreshold: The threshold for pixel count from range [0.0,1.0] before scaling both axes to a power of 2.
fields.CrunchCompressed: Whether the texture should be lossily compressed using Crunch. This goes on top of the compression set in PreferredFormat and doesn't decrease VRAM usage, only download size.
fields.MinSize: The miniumum size to show to the user, which scaled versions are generated by the cloud on the fly from the original and sent as part of the Asset Variant System
fields.MaxSize: The maxiumum size to show to the user, which scaled versions are generated by the cloud on the fly from the original and sent as part of the Asset Variant System
fields.KeepOriginalMipMaps: Whether to use the original mipmaps that came with the image data (yes you could have rainbow mipmaps at further distances) or not.
fields.MipMapFilter: How to apply filtering to the image's mipmaps. please see Filtering!
fields.Readable: Whether this can be read per pixel via things like ProtoFlux

methods.BleedColorToAlpha: extends the color data of the texture out into transparent areas from non transparent areas.
methods.MakeSquare: Resizes the image to make it square.
methods.TileLoop: Tiles the image X times on the width and height directions
methods.TileMirror: Tiles the image X times on the width and height directions, mirroring the image every other tiling.
methods.Rescale: Resizes the image up or down.
methods.Crop: Crops the image to a new area in pixels.
methods.Trim: Trims the image from the edges. for example, this can be used to trim a black and white mask image to get rid of extra white around the edges of the image.
methods.TrimTransparent: Trims the transparent areas off of the edges of an image.
methods.TrimByCornerColor: Stretches the image such that both sides are equal to the longest side
methods.KMeansCluster: This can be used to make the image into a vorroni version of it. is used to compress the color usage of an image to make it easily compressable. can also be used for art effects.
methods.Resize: Resizes the image up or down.
methods.ToNearestPOT: Resizes the image to the nearest power of two (2^x)
methods.GenerateBitmapMetadata: Generates the bitmap metadata (bitmaps are sets of smaller texture resolutions for performance) for this image.
methods.ReplaceFromClipboard: Replaces the image data with the data in the local user's clipboard
methods.InvertRGB: Inverts the colors of the image.
methods.InvertR: Inverts the red channel of the image.
methods.InvertG: Inverts the green channel of the image.
methods.InvertB: Inverts the blue channel of the image.
methods.InvertA: Inverts the alpha channel of the image.
methods.ColorToAlpha: Turns the color data of the image into transparency/alpha data.
methods.AlphaFromIntensity: Makes alpha/transparency data depending on the color intensity of the image.
methods.AlphaToMask: Turns the alpha of the image into a black and white image.
methods.RemoveAlpha: Removes the alpha data completely from the image or makes it white.
methods.GrayscaleAverage: Make grayscale image based on the average values for the colors per pixel.
methods.GrayscaleLuminance: Make grayscale image based on the color luminance per pixel.
methods.SwapRG: Swaps the red and green channels on the image
methods.SwapRB: Swaps the red and blue channels on the image
methods.SwapRA: Swaps the red and alpha channels on the image
methods.SwapGB: Swaps the green and blue channels on the image
methods.SwapGA: Swaps the green and alpha channels on the image
methods.SwapBA: Swaps the blue and alpha channels on the image
methods.AddBackground: adds a background of a color to a transparent image.
methods.AdjustGamma: Adjusts the gamma of the image.
methods.AdjustAlphaGamma: Adjusts the gamma of the alpha channel of the image.
methods.ShiftHue: Shifts the hue of HSV of the image.
methods.SetHue: Sets the Hue of HSV of the image.
methods.SetSaturation: Sets the saturation of HSV of the image.
methods.OffsetSaturation: Adds to the saturation of HSV of the image.
methods.MulSaturation: Multiplies the saturation of HSV of the image.
methods.SetValue: Sets the value of HSV of the image.
methods.MulValue: Multiplies the value of HSV of the image.
methods.OffsetValue: Adds to the value of HSV of the image.
methods.OffsetAlpha: Adds to the alpha of the image.
methods.Normalize: Normalizes the colors of the image, making it have a bigger color range usage.

usage: By default ``Filter`` is set to Bilinear Filtering, however Anisotropic Filtering results in a much better appearance at very steep angles, and only has a very minor performance impact. If visual fidelity of your texture is a concern, you should set ``Filter`` to Anisotropic, and ``AnisotropicLevel`` to 4 or 8.
